concerns.text: T018a (Batch aggregation) is listed AFTER T018 (Batch runner) but is a strict prerequisite for T028 (Simulation runner). The 'Dependencies & Execution Order' section correctly notes this, but the linear task list implies T018a can be done anytime after T018. T018a must be moved immediately after T018 and before any US2 tasks to ensure the global batch manifest exists before simulation begins.

T029a (Schema validation) is listed AFTER T029 (Serialization) in the task list, despite T029 explicitly depending on T029a ('ensuring schema compliance (T029a)'). T029a must precede T029 to prevent writing invalid data. The 'Dependencies' section notes this, but the task ordering violates the producer-consumer flow.

T035 (Sensitivity sweep) is listed AFTER T037 (Master analysis) and T036 (Plotting). T037 explicitly depends on T035 ('run tests (T033, T034, T035)'). T035 must be moved before T037. Similarly, T044 (Power analysis) is listed after T038 (Report generator) but is needed to validate SC-003 before final reporting; T044 should precede T038 or be a prerequisite for the final validation phase.

T045 (Full batch validation) depends on T035 (Sensitivity sweep) to check `sensitivity_sweep.json`, but T035 is listed after T045 in the task sequence. T045 cannot execute until T035 completes. T045 must be moved to the end of Phase 5, after T035 and T044.

T016a (Timeout utility) is marked [P] (parallel-safe) but is a hard dependency for T014 (Watts-Strogatz) and T018 (Batch runner). While the 'Notes' section clarifies this, marking it [P] is misleading as it cannot be executed in parallel with T014/T018 without risking race conditions or missing imports. The [P] tag should be removed or the task re-ordered to ensure T016a is completed before T014/T018.
